modalities.title: Acupuncture
modalities.shortDescriptionCore: A core Traditional Chinese Medicine modality using fine, sterile needles to support pain relief, stress regulation, and whole-body balance.
modalities.fullDescriptionCore: Acupuncture stimulates specific points to help regulate circulation, calm the nervous system, and support the body's natural recovery processes. Treatment plans are individualized based on your symptoms, health history, and response over time.
modalities.whatToExpectCore: Your visit includes intake, point selection, and a rest phase while needles are retained. Most patients report minimal discomfort and deep relaxation during treatment.

modalities.title: Chinese Herbal Medicine
modalities.shortDescriptionCore: Customized herbal formulas selected to match your presentation, constitution, and treatment goals.
modalities.fullDescriptionCore: Chinese herbal medicine combines multiple botanicals in balanced formulas. Prescriptions are adjusted as your condition changes, with attention to symptom patterns, digestion, sleep, and energy levels.
modalities.whatToExpectCore: After assessment, you receive a tailored formula and clear dosing guidance. Follow-up visits are used to monitor response and adjust the prescription when needed.

modalities.title: Cupping Therapy
modalities.shortDescriptionCore: Suction-based therapy used to release muscle tightness, support circulation, and ease stagnation-related discomfort.
modalities.fullDescriptionCore: Cupping uses negative pressure to mobilize soft tissue and improve local blood flow. Techniques may be static or moving, depending on your condition and comfort.
modalities.whatToExpectCore: You may feel a strong pull or pressure. Temporary circular marks can appear and usually fade over several days.

modalities.title: Moxibustion
modalities.shortDescriptionCore: Warming therapy using mugwort heat at targeted points to support circulation, energy, and cold-pattern conditions.
modalities.fullDescriptionCore: Moxibustion applies therapeutic heat to selected points to warm channels and support qi movement. It is commonly used for cold sensitivity, fatigue, and certain chronic patterns.
modalities.whatToExpectCore: You experience gentle, sustained warmth with close practitioner monitoring for comfort and safety.

modalities.title: Tui Na Medical Massage
modalities.shortDescriptionCore: Manual Chinese medicine bodywork combining acupressure and mobilization techniques for pain and function support.
modalities.fullDescriptionCore: Tui Na uses structured hand techniques along channels and affected areas to reduce tension, improve movement quality, and support rehabilitation goals.
modalities.whatToExpectCore: Treatment intensity can be adjusted from gentle to firm. Sessions are tailored to your tolerance and clinical goals.
benefitsCore: Helps reduce neck, shoulder, and back tension

modalities.title: Gua Sha
modalities.shortDescriptionCore: Instrument-assisted scraping technique used to release fascial tension and improve circulation in targeted regions.
modalities.fullDescriptionCore: Gua Sha applies repeated strokes with a smooth-edged tool to address tight tissue and stagnation. It is often selected for neck, shoulder, and upper back tension patterns.
modalities.whatToExpectCore: A lubricated area is treated with controlled strokes. Temporary redness may appear and usually resolves in a few days.

modalities.title: Chinese Dietary Therapy
modalities.shortDescriptionCore: Food-as-medicine guidance based on TCM pattern differentiation, constitution, and seasonal context.
modalities.fullDescriptionCore: Dietary recommendations are tailored to symptom pattern, digestion, and lifestyle realities. Plans focus on practical adjustments that are sustainable and culturally flexible.
modalities.whatToExpectCore: You receive clear food priorities, cooking-direction guidance, and simple habit updates that align with your treatment plan.
benefitsCore: Adapts to seasonal and individual needs

modalities.title: Lifestyle & Wellness Counseling
modalities.shortDescriptionCore: Structured guidance on sleep, stress, activity, and recovery habits to improve long-term treatment outcomes.
modalities.fullDescriptionCore: Lifestyle counseling translates TCM principles into practical routines for rest, pacing, movement, and stress regulation. Recommendations are personalized and adjusted as progress changes.
modalities.whatToExpectCore: You receive focused, actionable steps between visits with follow-up on adherence, barriers, and measurable progress.
